notes: v1.6.95 — NOW-TAB TRUTH (Item 1: coherence bug) + COMPLETION CARD REDESIGN (Item 2). ITEM 1 — the v1.6.92 coherence bug survived in a new form: on the NOW tab the ACTIVE BUILD card and the TODAY/completion card could show the SAME deployment name with CONTRADICTORY numbers (field evidence on v1.6.93: card '1 racks·0 done·0%' vs TODAY '1 DONE·100%'). Confirmed root cause = TWO independent resolvers (ACTIVE BUILD used activeContext_get(); TODAY used deploy_getActive()/most-recent) AND TWO counting bases (activeContext counts rack.status; the rollup counts via phase rollup) — they diverged on DUPLICATE-NAMED deployments (an old completed test dep + a new Master-scoped one sharing a name). FIX, all three layers: (1) ONE resolver — new nowtab_resolveDep() (deploy_getActive() first, most-recently-updated active fallback; deploy_getActive only wins when it is actually in the active set) feeds the WHOLE NOW tab. (2) ONE counting basis — both headline cards read counts from a SINGLE deploy_computeDeployRollup over that one record (removed the activeContext counts and the duplicate pct math; pct now declared once from rollup.pctOverall). (3) DISAMBIGUATION — the completion card shows the created date (formatDate) + '#'+last-4-of-id so duplicate names are distinguishable at a glance. The ACTIVE BUILD card's rack-level resume is guarded to only link a rack that belongs to the resolved deployment (no cross-deployment link from a stale activeContext). NO destructive ops — duplicates are surfaced, never auto-deleted/merged (operator's manual call per the spec). ITEM 2 — COMPLETION CARD REDESIGN: the old full-width card was a lone 96px ring in mostly-empty black space ('looks terrible' from the field). Now compact — ring LEFT (64px) + stat stack RIGHT (deployment name truncated + disambiguator, 'X of Y racks done', 'N/M phases', and a red BLOCKED badge when nonzero), ~half the old height, glanceable at arm's length. Reuses existing design tokens (orb/mono fonts, --hud-r12 card border, the STATIC 'badge b-red' for the blocked count) — NO new CSS (block-0 braces 2494, zero delta). The ring keeps the SVG arc (r=28; COMPLETE/BLOCKED label clears the stroke at all percentages); only a one-shot stroke-dashoffset transition + a static drop-shadow glow (no continuous pulse — prefers-reduced-motion respected); the card does NOT render at all when there is no deployment (no hollow card). VERIFIED: node --check 2/2 inline scripts + sw.js; JSON valid; style block untouched (block-0 braces 2494, no delta); 3-lens adversarial review (resolution coherence / card render / regression) — the bug is eliminated BY CONSTRUCTION, the new card is div-balanced (11/11), ring geometry correct, every downstream consumer + all removed-var references are clean (no ReferenceError), invariants hold (snapshot/freeze + EDP/vendor/reconcile/Master untouched, numbers all real from one rollup). POST-REVIEW: fixed the single real finding — the disambiguator's date half called fmtDate, which is a function-local inside shiftReport_generate (out of scope in today_render, so the date silently never rendered); repointed to the global formatDate. Browser end-to-end pending operator walkthrough (incl. the spec's profile-driven Austin proof + duplicate-name scenario). Built in Claude Code on fdb423a (v1.6.94); web-Claude does post-merge parity review (one-builder rule). Three stamps at v1.6.95.
